title: Aureline data-portability and customer-exit matrix row
description: Boundary schema for the artifact-domain rows seeded in artifacts/governance/portability_artifact_matrix.yaml and explained in docs/governance/data_portability_and_exit_matrix.md. A portability_row turns each managed, hybrid, or local-only artifact domain into an auditable contract: minimum export format, scriptability requirement, offboarding and deletion story, import unknown-field preservation rule, admin-versus-user scope, managed-versus-local dependency notes, and the staged-migration / self-host-move / air-gap / SaaS-cancellation rules that keep local-core workflows and export APIs truthful after service withdrawal. Adding a vocabulary value is additive-minor and bumps portability_row_schema_version; repurposing or reusing a value is breaking and opens a decision row.

portability_row_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the portability-row vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

path_ref.description: Repo-relative path or document reference. Consumers treat it as opaque text.

posture_note.description: Short, reviewable sentence clarifying a posture. Free-text and vague phrasing such as 'saved securely' are non-conforming.

artifact_domain_class.description: Closed set of artifact domains the matrix governs. Every domain that influences user exit, self-host move, or migration story must resolve to one of these classes. Adding a class is additive-minor and bumps portability_row_schema_version.

export_format_floor.description: Minimum default export format the row is contractually obliged to deliver. Open, text-friendly formats are preferred; binary or tarball formats are valid only when the artifact body genuinely requires them.

scriptability_requirement.description: Whether the export must be runnable from a documented CLI or API path without filing a support ticket. manual_export_only rows MUST cite the local recovery path; not_applicable rows MUST justify why scriptability is meaningless for the class.

admin_user_scope.description: Which actor may invoke the export and whose scope of data it covers. user_and_admin_distinct rows MUST keep the two scopes as separate exports; admin scope MUST NOT silently widen a user-initiated request.

managed_local_dependency_class.description: Dependency truth between local materialization and any managed copy. managed_only_no_local_form rows MUST disclose what the user loses on managed withdrawal; generated_packet_only rows describe artifacts that exist only when an export action assembles them.

import_unknown_field_rule.description: How an importer must treat fields it does not recognise. preserve_round_trip is the default for user-authored portable artifacts; reject_unknown_fields and not_importable rows MUST justify the closed posture in offboarding_notes.

deletion_story_class.description: How deletion completes for the domain. local_and_managed_delete_distinct rows MUST keep the two actions as separate operations with separate receipts; not_deletable_after_export is reserved for receipts and append-only ledgers.

offboarding_story_class.description: How the artifact participates in customer-exit. exit_packet_required rows MUST list the exit-packet fields that cite the row; manual_capture_outside_platform rows MUST name the local recovery path.

service_withdrawal_rule_class.description: Withdrawal scenario the rule covers. Every row MUST seed at least one rule per applicable scenario; managed_only_withdrawal is reserved for artifacts that cannot survive managed withdrawal and exists so the matrix surfaces the loss explicitly.

local_core_workflow_remains_truthful.description: Whether the documented local-core workflow remains valid after the scenario fires. False values MUST be paired with a manual-capture or exit-packet note; silent loss is non-conforming.

export_api_remains_truthful.description: Whether the documented export API or scriptable path keeps the same shape after the scenario fires. False values MUST cite the surface that takes over and the migration window.

manual_capture_required.description: Whether the scenario forces a manual local capture step the user must perform before access ends. Default false; surfaces MUST disclose this when true.

rule_notes.description: Optional additional notes for the rule (mirrors, parity windows, replay options, etc.).

service_withdrawal_rules.description: Per-scenario rules covering staged-migration, self-host move, air-gap, SaaS cancellation, and managed-only withdrawal. The same scenario value MUST NOT appear twice in one row.

additional_export_formats.description: Additional formats the surface MAY emit beyond the floor; the floor itself is mandatory.

manifest_required.description: Whether a separate manifest is required to call the export interpretable. Embedded-only exports without a manifest are non-conforming for hybrid or managed rows.

raw_secret_excluded_required.description: Whether the export MUST assert raw_secret_excluded per the storage-and-retention vocabulary. true is the default for any row that touches credential or token references.

round_trip_supported.description: Whether export-then-import on the same Aureline build is contractually a no-op for user-authored fields. False values MUST cite the import_notes that explain the asymmetry.

import_notes.description: Notes about importer behavior (downgrade rules, fidelity caveats, etc.).

scope_notes.description: Notes about admin-vs-user scope or managed-vs-local dependency rules the row inherits.

local_and_managed_actions_are_distinct.description: Whether deleting the local materialization is a separate action from deleting any managed copy. MUST be true on rows whose managed_local_dependency is anything but local_only.

completion_evidence.description: Evidence vocabulary that MUST agree with the record-class registry's completion_evidence enum.

deletion_notes.description: Notes about delete behavior (legal-hold blockers, retained-subset reasoning, etc.).

support_ticket_required.description: Whether obtaining the export currently requires filing a support ticket. The acceptance bar is false for at least one fixture per major export class; surfaces that fail this MUST cite the decision row that justifies the dependency.

offboarding_notes.description: Offboarding-specific notes the exit-packet builder or admin export UI must preserve.

record_class_refs.description: record_class_id values from artifacts/governance/record_class_registry.yaml that constrain the row. Every row MUST cite at least one record class so retention and hold posture is inherited rather than re-declared.

storage_mode_refs.description: Consumer ids from artifacts/governance/storage_modes.yaml whose default credential / artifact / retention modes the row inherits.

source_docs.description: Repo docs a reviewer should consult when changing the row.

schema_refs.description: Optional downstream boundary schemas associated with the row.

deployment_profile_refs.description: Optional deployment_profile_id values whose withdrawal scenarios the row's rules cover.

title.description: Short human-readable title for the row.

description.description: One-paragraph statement of which artifact class the row covers and what it does not.

notes.description: Optional additional note for future consumers.
